topic.what: Comprehensive review of acupuncture as a treatment for chronic gastritis, focusing on three mechanistic pillars: vagal neuromodulation, anti-inflammatory cytokine regulation, and enhancement of gastric motility.
topic.how: Narrative and systematic literature synthesis drawing on human and animal studies; mechanistic pathways traced from needle stimulation at specific acupoints through autonomic nervous system responses to gastric mucosal outcomes.
topic.why_matters: Establishes a neurophysiological rationale for acupuncture efficacy in gastritis, moving beyond empirical TCM justification toward testable biomedical mechanisms.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Clarity of proposed mechanism linking needle stimulation to gastric mucosal outcomes
characteristics.value: Three discrete mechanisms identified with supporting literature; vagal pathway best characterized
characteristics.vs_baseline: Earlier reviews relied on traditional qi/meridian explanations without neural correlates
characteristics.evidence: Review of neurophysiology and immunology studies
characteristics.context: Vagal anti-inflammatory reflex (cholinergic pathway) has the strongest cross-species evidence

characteristics.what_it_measures: Strength of evidence for acupuncture-induced changes in pro-inflammatory cytokines (IL-6, TNF-alpha, IL-1beta)
characteristics.value: Consistent reduction in IL-6 and TNF-alpha reported across multiple studies
characteristics.vs_baseline: Compared to sham acupuncture controls where reported
characteristics.evidence: Aggregated RCT and animal study data
characteristics.context: Effect sizes vary; blinding challenges in acupuncture RCTs limit confidence

good_for: Integrative medicine researchers designing acupuncture + pharmacotherapy combination studies
Neurogastroenterology researchers studying gut-brain axis in inflammatory conditions
Clinicians seeking evidence-based rationale for referral to acupuncture for functional dyspepsia or chronic gastritis
not_for: Acute H. pylori eradication (antibiotics remain first-line)
Patients with coagulopathy or acupoint-region skin infections
requires: Familiarity with autonomic nervous system anatomy
Understanding of cytokine signaling in mucosal immunity

novelty: Unified mechanistic framework combining neuromodulation, cytokine regulation, and motility under a single review
Explicit mapping of vagal cholinergic anti-inflammatory reflex to acupoint stimulation in gastric disease
open_problems: Optimal acupoint selection, needle depth, and stimulation frequency for each mechanism remain undefined
Long-term durability of motility and anti-inflammatory effects post-treatment course unknown
Mechanistic studies in human gastric biopsies (vs. animal models) remain scarce
